title: Smoky Roasted Polish Pork Gratin
description: A hearty, umami-rich dish featuring a crispy snappy crust and tender pork from the comfort of your kitchen. Inspired by traditional monsoon-seasoned Polish cuisine.
instructions: Season the pork knuckle with smoked paprika, black pepper, and garlic powder.
In a large pan over medium heat, cook the chopped onion until translucent.
Add the caraway seeds and cook for 1 minute.
Add the sliced potatoes and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Transfer the potato mixture to a greased 9x13-inch baking dish.
Place the pork knuckle on top of the potatoes.
Dot the top of the pork with butter.
In a small bowl, mix the flour and heavy cream until smooth.
Pour the cream mixture over the pork and potatoes.
Sprinkle the grated cheddar cheese over the top.
Bake in the preheated oven for 1 hour and 20 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the pork is cooked through.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve hot.
